content.problemStatement:
In Chefland, each chicken has XXX legs and each duck has YYY legs. Chef's farm can have **exactly one type** of bird.

Given that the birds on the farm have a total of ZZZ legs:


Print `CHICKEN`, if the farm can have only chickens but not ducks.
Print `DUCK`, if the farm can have only ducks but not chickens.
Print `ANY`, if the farm can have either chickens or ducks.
Print `NONE`, if the farm can have neither chickens nor ducks.

Input Format

The first line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing three space-separated integers X,Y,ZX, Y, ZX,Y,Z.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the type of bird on the farm.



Print `CHICKEN`, if the farm can have only chickens but not ducks.
Print `DUCK`, if the farm can have only ducks but not chickens.
Print `ANY`, if the farm can have either chickens or ducks.
Print `NONE`, if the farm can have neither chickens nor ducks.

You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings AnY\texttt{AnY}AnY, anY\texttt{anY}anY, any\texttt{any}any and ANY\texttt{ANY}ANY will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers X, Y and Z – the number of legs of a chicken, the number of legs of a duck and the total number of legs on the farm respectively.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output one word on its own line:
CHICKEN – if Z is divisible by X but not by Y.
DUCK – if Z is divisible by Y but not by X.
ANY – if Z is divisible by both X and Y.
NONE – if Z is divisible by neither X nor Y.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 5 is not divisible by 2 nor by 3 → NONE.\nTest case 2: 2 is divisible by both 2 and 2 → ANY.\nTest case 3: 6 is divisible by 3 (chicken) but not by 4 (duck) → CHICKEN.
First case: 20 is divisible by both 5 and 10 → ANY.\nSecond case: 14 is divisible by 7 (chicken) only → CHICKEN.
14 is not a multiple of 4 nor of 6, so the farm cannot consist of a single bird type → NONE.

solution.approach:
For each test case we only need to check whether the total number of legs Z can be formed using a single bird type. The farm can contain only chickens if Z is a multiple of X (i.e., Z % X == 0). It can contain only ducks if Z is a multiple of Y (Z % Y == 0). Evaluate both conditions and output according to the four possible combinations.
The check is O(1) per test case, so the whole solution runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used
